com.mentor.chs.plugin

IXStyleableTable, IXSubAssemblyNameConstraint, IXSubAssemblyPartNumberConstraint, IXSymbolAction, IXSymbolApplicationAction, IXTable, IXTask, IXTeamcenterReportExchangeRegistrar, IXTerminateShieldToPigtailConstraint, IXTopologyAction, IXTopologyAutoLink, IXTopologyBridgeProcessor, IXTopologyDesignTabPanel, IXTopologyDRCheck, IXTopologyInspectionPanel, IXTopologyMinSpliceSeparationConstraint, IXTopologyPlugin, IXTopologyReport, IXTopologyRoutingConstraint, IXUserAccessibilityEvaluation<O>, IXWireAttributeConstraint, IXWireRoutingConstraint, IXWireTerminalSpecConstraint, IXWorkbookProvider<D>

    @NotToBeImplementedDirectly
public interface IXPlugin
    This is the parent interface for all plug-in interfaces. Plug-ins should not implement this interface directly.

    WARNING: When implementing plugins NEVER NEVER EVER exit the application. 

        System.exit(); // This is BAD

    This will cause the application to exit in a very untidy way and will result in the loss of any unsaved data. DO NOT DO THIS.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      java.lang.String getDescription()
      Get the description of the plug-in.
      java.lang.String getName()
      Get the name of the plug-in.
      java.lang.String getVersion()
      Get the version of the plug-in.

    • Method Detail

      • getDescription

        java.lang.String getDescription()
        Get the description of the plug-in.

        This should return a human readable description of what the plug-in does. This text will be made available to users so they can decide if this is the plug-in they want to use.

        Returns:
        the description of the plug-in.

      • getName

        java.lang.String getName()
        Get the name of the plug-in.

        This should return a human readable name of the plug-in. This text will be made available to users so they can decide if this is the plug-in they want to use.

        Returns:
        the name of the plug-in.

      • getVersion

        java.lang.String getVersion()
        Get the version of the plug-in.

        This should return a human readable version string for the plug-in. This text will be made available to users you can ensure that they have the correct version of the plug-in. If you change the plug-in for any reason, you should update the version string.

        Returns:
        the version string.
